req.title: KYC refresh
req.statement: A customer shall complete a KYC refresh every 365 days; accounts beyond refresh window shall be restricted to inbound transactions only.

doc.reasoning: R-FIN-015 mandates a 365-day KYC refresh cycle. Key partitions are: (1) customer within refresh window submitting valid KYC data — expect 200 + verified=true; (2) customer with failed compliance data — expect 403; (3) boundary at exactly day 365 — last valid day for refresh, expect 200 + verified=true; (4) boundary at day 366 — account restricted to inbound only; (5) state transition from unrefreshed to refreshed. Five tests cover EP (valid submission), NEG (compliance failure), BVA (day 364 still valid), BVA (day 366 restricted), and STC (account restriction lifted after refresh).

tests.name: test_kyc_refresh_valid_submission_returns_verified
preconditions: Customer is authenticated with a valid JWT
Customer last KYC was completed fewer than 365 days ago
KYC refresh form payload is compliant
actions: POST /kyc/refresh with valid compliant KYC payload and Authorization header
expected: Response body contains refreshed timestamp within last 5 seconds
trace: R-FIN-015: customer shall complete KYC refresh every 365 days
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import datetime, timezone

def test_kyc_refresh_valid_submission_returns_verified(api_client, valid_kyc_payload, auth_headers):
    response = api_client.post("/kyc/refresh", json=valid_kyc_payload, headers=auth_headers)
    assert response.status_code == 200
    body = response.json()
    assert body["customer"]["verified"] is True
    refreshed_at = datetime.fromisoformat(body["refreshed"].replace("Z", "+00:00"))
    now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
    delta = (now - refreshed_at).total_seconds()
    assert 0 <= delta < 5, f"Unexpected refreshed timestamp delta: {delta}s"

tests.name: test_kyc_refresh_compliance_failure_returns_403
preconditions: Customer is authenticated with a valid JWT
KYC refresh payload contains data that will fail compliance checks (e.g. flagged identity)
actions: POST /kyc/refresh with non-compliant KYC payload and Authorization header
expected: Response body contains error code indicating compliance failure
trace: R-FIN-015: error pathway 403 when submission fails compliance
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_kyc_refresh_compliance_failure_returns_403(api_client, noncompliant_kyc_payload, auth_headers):
    response = api_client.post("/kyc/refresh", json=noncompliant_kyc_payload, headers=auth_headers)
    assert response.status_code == 403
    body = response.json()
    assert "error" in body
    assert body["error"]["code"] in ("COMPLIANCE_FAILURE", "KYC_REJECTED")
    if "customer" in body:
        assert body["customer"]["verified"] is not True

tests.name: test_kyc_refresh_on_day_364_is_accepted
preconditions: Customer is authenticated with a valid JWT
Customer last KYC was completed exactly 364 days ago (one day before boundary)
actions: Seed customer record with last_kyc_date = today - 364 days
POST /kyc/refresh with valid compliant KYC payload and Authorization header
trace: R-FIN-015: accounts beyond 365-day refresh window restricted; day 364 is within window
BVA lower boundary — one day before restriction triggers
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import date, timedelta

def test_kyc_refresh_on_day_364_is_accepted(
    api_client, valid_kyc_payload, auth_headers, seed_kyc_date
):
    last_date = date.today() - timedelta(days=364)
    seed_kyc_date(last_date)
    response = api_client.post("/kyc/refresh", json=valid_kyc_payload, headers=auth_headers)
    assert response.status_code == 200
    body = response.json()
    assert body["customer"]["verified"] is True

tests.name: test_kyc_refresh_on_day_366_account_restricted_to_inbound
preconditions: Customer is authenticated with a valid JWT
Customer last KYC was completed exactly 366 days ago (one day past boundary)
actions: Seed customer record with last_kyc_date = today - 366 days
POST /kyc/refresh with valid compliant KYC payload and Authorization header
Attempt an outbound transaction via POST /transactions
expected: KYC refresh POST returns 200 and verified=true
Outbound transaction before refresh is rejected with HTTP 403 or 423
After successful refresh, outbound transaction is permitted
trace: R-FIN-015: accounts beyond 365-day window restricted to inbound transactions only
BVA upper boundary — one day past restriction threshold
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import date, timedelta

def test_kyc_refresh_on_day_366_account_restricted_to_inbound(
    api_client, valid_kyc_payload, outbound_tx_payload, auth_headers, seed_kyc_date
):
    last_date = date.today() - timedelta(days=366)
    seed_kyc_date(last_date)
    pre_tx = api_client.post("/transactions", json=outbound_tx_payload, headers=auth_headers)
    assert pre_tx.status_code in (403, 423), f"Expected restriction, got {pre_tx.status_code}"
    refresh_resp = api_client.post("/kyc/refresh", json=valid_kyc_payload, headers=auth_headers)
    assert refresh_resp.status_code == 200
    assert refresh_resp.json()["customer"]["verified"] is True
    post_tx = api_client.post("/transactions", json=outbound_tx_payload, headers=auth_headers)
    assert post_tx.status_code in (200, 201), f"Expected success post-refresh, got {post_tx.status_code}"

tests.name: test_kyc_refresh_state_transition_unverified_to_verified
preconditions: Customer is authenticated with a valid JWT
Customer account is in UNVERIFIED state (verified=false, KYC not yet refreshed)
actions: Confirm initial state: GET /customers/me returns verified=false
POST /kyc/refresh with valid compliant KYC payload
Confirm final state: GET /customers/me returns verified=true
expected: State transition UNVERIFIED -> VERIFIED is recorded
trace: R-FIN-015: trigger refresh form submitted transitions customer to verified state
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_kyc_refresh_state_transition_unverified_to_verified(
    api_client, valid_kyc_payload, auth_headers, seed_unverified_customer
):
    seed_unverified_customer()
    pre = api_client.get("/customers/me", headers=auth_headers)
    assert pre.status_code == 200
    assert pre.json()["customer"]["verified"] is False, "Precondition: customer must be unverified"
    refresh = api_client.post("/kyc/refresh", json=valid_kyc_payload, headers=auth_headers)
    assert refresh.status_code == 200
    post = api_client.get("/customers/me", headers=auth_headers)
    assert post.status_code == 200
    assert post.json()["customer"]["verified"] is True
